import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go

# Select a categorical feature
print("All categorical feature: ")
print(data.select_dtypes(include=[object]).columns.to_list())

feature = 'Location'
print("Feature observed: ", feature)

# Get value counts
value_counts = data[feature].value_counts()

# Create bar chart
fig = go.Figure(data=[go.Bar(
    x=value_counts.values,
    y=value_counts.index,
    orientation='h',
    marker_color='#4facfe',
    text=value_counts.values,
    textposition='outside'
)])

fig.update_layout(
    title=f"{feature.replace("_", " ").title()} Distribution",
    xaxis_title='Count',
    yaxis_title=feature.replace("_", " ").title(),
    width=800,
    height=800,
    yaxis={'autorange': 'reversed'}
)

fig.show()

# Statistics
print(f"
{feature} Statistics")
print(f"Unique values: {data[feature].nunique()}")
print(f"Missing: {data[feature].isnull().sum()} ({data[feature].isnull().sum() / len(data) * 100 :.2f}%)")
print(f"
Top categories:")
for cat, count in value_counts.head(10).items():
    print(f"{cat}: {count:,} ({count / len(data) * 100 :.2f}%)")
